description: When selecting different voices in the settings tab, we should have the voice read a brief bit of text so that you can quickly preview the voice before deciding on one.

results.identified_issue: The voice picker used a navigationLink Picker (push a list, tap to select, pop back), which makes auditioning several voices tedious and gives no audio. I replaced it with an inline tappable list where each row selects the voice AND plays a brief sample. Audio goes through a new lightweight VoicePreviewPlayer that renders the sample with Kokoro (SpeechRenderer) when the weights are installed — the only path where the eleven speakers actually sound different — and falls back to AVSpeechSynthesizer when they aren't (footer warns the fallback sounds the same for every voice). I kept the preview player separate from ClipPreviewPlayer rather than reusing it because the feed player carries scrubber/completion/no-forward-seek rules that don't apply to a one-shot audition. Alternative considered: adding a play button per row separate from selection, but the request framed it as 'when selecting different voices... preview,' so I tied preview to selection. Could not build/run to verify on device (build is vigil's job); SourceKit 'cannot find in scope' diagnostics are false positives from the new file not yet being in the regenerated Xcode project.
results.summary: Tapping a voice in Settings now plays a short spoken sample so you can preview it before choosing
results.detailed_description: When you pick a voice in Settings it now reads a one-line sample aloud in that voice, so you can hear each speaker before committing. What was wrong: the Voice picker just pushed a plain checklist with no audio, so the only way to compare voices was to select one, wait for the next card in the feed, and repeat. What I changed: the Voice section is now an inline list — tap any row and it (1) selects that voice and (2) immediately speaks a short sample ('Hi, I'm Aria. Here's how I'll sound reading your feed.') in it. A small speaker icon on each row animates while that voice is sounding and shows a spinner while Kokoro renders the first sample; a checkmark marks the current choice; tapping the playing voice again stops it. The sample uses the neural Kokoro voice when the model is installed (the only way the different speakers truly sound distinct); until the download finishes it falls back to the system voice and the footer says so, since that fallback sounds identical for every speaker. Preview stops automatically when you close the sheet or switch to the system engine. How I proved it: code review and reasoning through the render/playback paths — I reused the same SpeechRenderer/AVAudioSession approach the feed player uses. I could not run it on-device myself (that's vigil's build/deploy step), so the actual sound and the render latency for the first sample are still unverified on hardware.
